name: The House of Da Vinci VR

reviews.review_description:
First of all, if you like Myst, or Riven, or The Room VR, or The 7th Guest, then this game is made for you. The artists have taken the adventure game genre to an absolute apex. The first few sections of the game are pretty good, but mostly standard graphics. Then there's a section in a graveyard and my mind was blown by the sound, visuals and puzzles there. This team knows how to cook! The walls have bump maps and great textures that make it look old and detailed. There is one part of a puzzle that has stone, and the outside of the stone is polished and shiny, and the cut inside actually looks dull, powdery with little flecks of gold in places! I mean, holy cow, the attention to detail left me breathless. If you can, resist the temptation to press the hint button! You can do it and it will come to you eventually. In the meantime, you can just look around at the gorgeous environment as you contemplate life. It was really only the final level, level 8, where sadly I ended up taking some hints.

Side note, there's a menu screen where you start the game. It's just outside Leonardo's house, and at first it is night time and later it is raining. Just wow! The water ripples on the cobblestone walkway, and rain drops splash and ripple the water. I turned around and water rivulets were dripping down the side of the wall and door. It's just such incredible artistry by the makers of this game. Oh but I'm sad that the sound effects for the rainy menu scene did not kick in. Instead, it was playing the default crickets and dogs barking sounds, but it was weird that I couldn't hear the rain or thunder. I know they have the sound effect because level 6 has it in the armory. Too bad they didn't put them in for the menu scene.

Finally, the torch flames and curling smoke have never looked this good in any VR game. These artists are at the height of their powers. The people who made this game are, like Da Vinci himself, masters of their craft.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for playing and for taking your time to provide such a thorough and wonderful feedback. All the best!

reviews.review_description:
I really wanted to like this game and eagerly awaited its release.
I have found the controls really frustrating.  Jerky movements and imprecise gripping of objects.
The hints system is useless and i have had to google tips on how to get through sections because the controls were so clunky.  Needs a major update,.

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving the game a try and for sharing detailed feedback. Sorry to hear the experience felt frustrating for you, especially after looking forward to the release. If you’d like to send us more specifics about the situations you encountered, our support team would be happy to take a closer look.

reviews.review_description: The puzzles in the game are very good - neither too difficult nor too easy, just fun - the graphics are excellent for Quest 3, the developers have done a fantastic job. I will be eagerly awaiting sequel 2 and 3, as well as your new project, The House of Tesla, from your flat-screen games. Keep up the good work team!

Smart puzzles and great visuals. Only issue was story was only text based and ending too obscure. It it wasn't for the title of the level in level select I would never know it was a time machine. What of the flying contraption at the begining?

Side note I almost laughed hysterically at the canon contraption design in that one level. Is that the mechina magnifica from the davinchi episode of futurama?
 😂

I am almost 60 and have played quite a few games over the years. I have purchased quite a few meta games over the last 3-4 years I have had my Quest 2. I purchased a Quest 3 a couple months ago and with this game the graphics are incredible. I must say it is the only game in the last 10 years that I could not wait to get back into it. There was about 3 spots where I had to use the hints but resist and look around. Well worth the purchase price. Sometimes you purchase a game and wonder if the value was there. Well I feel confident that I made the right decision to purchase this one. Look forward to more like it. Thanks Blue Brain

The House of Da Vinci VR feels crafted with genuine artistry and respect for the player’s intelligence. From the moment you step into 16th‑century Florence, the game surrounds you with beautifully realized environments inspired by Leonardo’s inventions and artwork. The sense of presence is remarkable — the mechanical puzzles, lockboxes, and intricate devices feel tactile, logical, and deeply satisfying to solve.


The standout feature for me is the time‑viewing device, which lets you peek into the past to uncover clues and hidden mechanisms. It adds a clever narrative twist and makes puzzle‑solving feel layered and rewarding.


Visually, the game is breathtaking. Textures, lighting, and environmental details are all meticulously crafted, from the weathered stonework to the atmospheric Renaissance interiors. The VR immersion is top‑tier, and the world feels alive and authentic thanks to the developers’ clear passion for Da Vinci’s work and the era he lived in.


👍 A few small areas for improvement
These didn’t diminish my enjoyment, but they’re worth mentioning:


1. Some interaction points sit too low when playing seated. A few puzzles required me to reset to a standing position because certain levers or action points ended up inside my couch while seated. A small adjustment to seated‑mode interaction height would make the experience smoother.


2. A couple of puzzles hinge on very tiny details. I found myself stuck once or twice simply because I missed a subtle element. The game rewards thoroughness, but at times it requires the player to be exhaustively meticulous in touching and manipulating every possible component.


3. The ending cinematic sequences felt a bit rushed and obscure. 


Even with these minor quirks, this is an exceptional VR puzzle adventure like Myst, The Room VR, or The 7th Guest — this is absolutely worth it.

I felt like it really just dropped you into a story I wasn't really following, but I get the main aim of the game is the puzzle. Feels amazing when you get the concept of a puzzle and even better when you solve one. There is a built in hint system but I have to confess I did have a YouTube browser open for one part of one puzzle. I'm sorry all!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the great review. That feeling when a puzzle finally clicks is exactly what we hoped players would experience. And don’t worry, we’ve all needed a little extra help on a puzzle at some point 😄 Really glad you enjoyed the journey.

reviews.review_description:
Well into the game, a canon must be fired.  I am unable to grip the firing lever.  The grip function has worked in the game until this exercise.  My controller batteries are powered at 80%. There is plenty of light. I am well within my room scale boundary. ￼ The controllers are fully functional in other apps, such as Red Matter, Metro Awakening and Walkabout Mini Golf.￼ I have used search engines to explore causes and resolution. I cannot find a resolution so I must exit the game permanently.  However, up until this point, I’ve had a very pleasant experience. I regret not being able to finish.
UPDATE June16 2026.  Kept trying.  Thanx to Developer for kind reply.  Took the headset outside in shade and drew the biggest boundary. Made many attempts to grasp firing mechanism. Then the miracle happened!  The canon fired hitting the final swinging ball!  The rest of the game was magic. —. So much immersion coupled with challenge.  This game is SFA (Simply Freaking Amazing)!  I am so glad that I persisted.  Many thanks to the gifted creators—you are AWESOME.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the detailed feedback and for spending time with the game. We’re really glad to hear you were enjoying the experience up to that point. Please feel free to contact our support team with a few more details about the issue, we’d be happy to look into it with you and hopefully help you continue the adventure.

reviews.review_title: I've played a lot of puzzle games
reviews.review_description: This is, by far, the best puzzle game I've played to date! The graphics are outstanding. I find myself sometimes, just standing and looking around to throughly enjoy the surroundings before even starting to solve the puzzles. Incredible!

Absolutely the best game I played until now. Great work in obtaining the best possible graphics for the VR.  A lot of complex problems to be solved but in case of need a little help is always possible

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the amazing review. Really happy to hear you enjoyed both the visuals and the puzzles, the team put a huge amount of work into both. Glad the hint system helped keep the adventure moving when needed 😊

reviews.review_description: I’m not a Puzzle / Escape Room game person, I get frustrated extremely fast and boil over if I’m stuck at a point with no sense how to progress. That said I warmed up to this one. The Da Vinci style wood-metal mechanics were intriguing and the general acoustics and visual atmosphere of the game kept me coming back. Good value for money. Good job team!

I really wanted to like this game, and in many ways I actually do. The atmosphere, the visuals, and the overall concept are genuinely impressive, especially in VR. It feels like stepping into a beautifully crafted mechanical world inspired by Da Vinci’s imagination.

However, this is also one of the most demanding puzzle experiences I’ve played. The puzzles themselves are not illogical — on the contrary, they are often very clever and well designed — but they can go extremely far in their complexity, with multiple layers and connections that are not always easy to follow.

I enjoy puzzle games a lot, but here the experience sometimes became overwhelming. There were many moments where I felt like I was constantly going back and forth between different mechanisms, and after a while it started to feel more exhausting than engaging.

The hint system helps, but especially towards the end, it sometimes gives you the solution without really explaining the reasoning behind it. In those cases, I found myself knowing what to do, but not understanding why, which was frustrating. I even had to look up explanations online to fully grasp the logic behind certain sequences, and I noticed I’m not alone in that.

Another major issue for me was the VR interaction and movement system. The teleport-based movement can make positioning frustrating, especially when playing seated. You often end up slightly too close or too far from objects, and there’s no fine adjustment to compensate. This becomes even more problematic when precise gestures are required.

Speaking of interactions, some actions feel overly sensitive or unclear. There were multiple times where I had the correct idea and performed the right motion, but the game didn’t register it properly. In a game that is already very challenging, this adds an extra layer of frustration that could be avoided.

I also think the experience would benefit from more accessibility options: the ability to reset a puzzle instantly, or even skip certain puzzles after struggling for too long. When challenge turns into frustration, it risks pushing players away instead of rewarding them.

Finally, the ending felt surprisingly abrupt. After so many hours of effort and concentration, it just stops rather suddenly, leaving things unresolved. I understand this might be intentional, but it left me more frustrated than intrigued.

That said, I can absolutely see why some players love this game. If you enjoy very intricate puzzles, careful observation, and don’t mind spending a long time experimenting and backtracking, this will be a great experience for you.

For me, it was ultimately more frustrating than rewarding — and from what I’ve seen, I’m not entirely alone in feeling that way — but I still respect the craftsmanship behind it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the detailed feedback. We’re glad you appreciated the atmosphere and design. We hear you on puzzle complexity, hints, and VR controls. This helps us improve. Sorry it felt frustrating at times, but we truly value your input!

reviews.review_description:
I really dig the davinci wooden contraptions, felt great interacting with them. The puzzles are usually in my sweet spot, interesting but straightforward. There were few that left me feeling "how should I've guessed this??" (looking at you, Fibonacci numbers), but that's the nature of the genre.

As others have said, the seated gameplay was positioned too close, so I had to manually go back with the chair after repositioning. After that it was mostly fine... some crank bars were so low, I had to reach below my knees to grab it. Maybe an artificial crouch toggle would help there?

My pet peeve with the game was that after every level, it throws you back to the menu to show you your time. It is an immersion breaker, just start the next level!
The other one is teleporting to a puzzle in a bad angle. I would expect that the puzzle should be right in front of me, not eg behind me. I know you can adjust the angle, but since you need to push the stick forward to teleport, there is not much room adjust the angle.

Still 5/5

developer_response.body: Really appreciate you taking the time to write this. Glad you enjoyed it and had a good experience with the game. We've also noted the pet peeve you mentioned – thanks for noting that!

reviews.review_title: Fab game for sim sickness sufferers!
reviews.review_description: This game had me hooked from puzzle 1! I'm always a bit wary about getting new games because I suffer with the dreaded simulator sickness but the way you move around this game doesnt affect it at all! Played it to the end and loved every minute!
Awesome game

I read some negative reviews of people that were frustrated about various puzzles like the catapult where they couldnt figure it out. So i took the challenge and solved all the puzzles that they couldnt hahaa! Now i feel so superior to half these people . Bow down to me.  This game would of been 5 stars  because its a great game and visuals are 5/5 but im giving it four mainly because the UI is a little bit clunky so you got to align your hand perfectly with the objects sometimes causing you to miss a puzzle and when you look at the hints your like “i swiped” that and it didnt do anything. Either way its a great game similar to the room vr

The House of Da Vinci VR is a very good game with excellent graphics. The environments are beautifully detailed, and the Renaissance atmosphere feels immersive and authentic. The Leonardo da Vinci theme couldn’t be more fitting for a puzzle-based experience.

The puzzles range from easy to moderately challenging, and some are genuinely difficult. A few of them, however, feel like they lack clear logic at first glance, which can be frustrating.

My biggest complaint is the controls when playing seated. They are quite poor. You are always positioned too close to the puzzle, and you can’t move back to get a better perspective. This makes it harder to use tools properly. Also, when you need to move levers located below you, it becomes very uncomfortable. Since you’re so close, your virtual legs block the movement, forcing you into awkward positions just to interact with the mechanism. It would also be great to have the option to play while lying down, with better camera adjustment and positioning flexibility.

Overall, it’s a great experience visually and thematically, but it clearly works better if played standing up.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing and for the honest review, Scolome!

reviews.review_description:
First off, this game is stunning! The details, the textures, everything is beautiful. Unfortunately, all movement is teleportation to a few key locations per level, so you can't admire all the details up close.
Second, this is a puzzler. Old puzzles, new puzzler, easy puzzles, hard puzzles, puzzles just to unlock other puzzles. Some you have to skip and come back to later. These are explicit, in your face, unapologetic, undisguised puzzles. If you are looking for subtler puzzlers and a storyline, like in Cyan games or Red Matter, you will be disappointed.
Third, the game has 3 level of hints, and you should use all 3 whenever you need to, because without at least the first 2, you have no chance of solving some puzzles. The third level will keep you on track, especially when you have to transform an item in inventory in some subtle way that you never suspected. And sometimes, you just have to break down and follow a walkthru. 
So if you like puzzles, and know how to get around stumpers without getting frustrated, this is a gorgeous way to spend hours in VR.

reviews.review_title: Another First Class game! Buy it!!
reviews.review_description: Update :I've just finished it. Buy this game! Really excellent game with a variety of interesting puzzles set in 16th century Florence. Excellent atmosphere. At times when manipulating objects your hands get stuck when you want to remove them. But it's not a big deal. Definitely recommend!

This is a really cool game. Very beautiful and realistic graphics and unique and fun puzzles to complete. I would’ve loved more hints in the environments for the puzzles that don’t have a clear way to solve. Hated to have to hit the hints button so much. More rooms please!
Hope you bring all the other House of Da Vinci to Meta VR
The worst ice played so far. Navigation is brutal and the fact that the first few steps are nearly impossible to figure out without hints is ridiculous. Go back to the drawing board.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to play and leave feedback. Noted on your experience with the early part of the game. If you ever want to share more specific details, our support team is happy to look into it. Appreciate you giving it a try.

reviews.review_description: One of the first few games I’ve tried, and now expectations are very high. Amazed by how realistic and challenging each chapter is. Definitely worth buying!

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing and for such a positive review!

reviews.review_description: We really enjoyed the puzzles in this game, but seemed very fast. The ending was a bit of a bummer, but overall was a good game and would recommend if you enjoy puzzle games.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for playing and for the thoughtful feedback. Really glad you enjoyed the puzzles and overall experience, even if it felt a bit short in the end. We appreciate the recommendation and you taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_title: A bit short but otheewise amazing game
reviews.review_description: Beautifully designed and very well made. I just wish it was a bit longer (especially since replayability is basically zero). I hope they publish a sequel soon 😊

developer_response.body: Really appreciate this, thank you. Glad the experience worked well for you and you had fun with it, albeit shorter than expected.

reviews.review_description:
Awesome graphics, atmosphere and puzzles. 

Not super difficult so it’s fun to play. 

Recommend!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the kind words and support. Really glad you enjoyed the game and had a good time with it.

reviews.review_title: What VR was meant to be
reviews.review_description: This was an amazing game. Great visuals, challenging puzzles, great story line.  This is exactly what I want to see more of!!

developer_response.body: That’s great to hear, thanks for playing. Always nice to know the game connected with players like you.

reviews.review_description:
What can I say about this game ! .
I'm an older player and whoever thought this up is pure genius .
I've so enjoyed playing this , the puzzles are very complex and keep you thinking , putting it down and going  back to it  to complete a bit more , storyline is great too .
I can highly recommend this to anyone who loves puzzles , and great graphics .
I can't wait for more of Blue Brain games .
Congratulations guys on a truly wonderful gaming experience .
10 / 10 .

Just wish you could walk around and interact more but this is a teleport game, focused on some pretty impressive mechanical puzzles. The environment is so rich and graphics so sharp! 
I didn’t like how it ended tho :(

The graphics are great 
The puzzle's are brilliant 
I'm an older person at 66 yrs old I'm not generally interested in shooting games so go for either simulator or puzzle games... 
This game definitely comes up to this with ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐out of 5.... Love the whole package including the game play, menu settings and the help, hint options... Definitely keeping  my brain active.... Great work Devs...
Didn't take long to complete it.I thought it there would be more to it

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing The House of Da Vinci VR and for sharing your thoughts. We’ll keep working hard to bring more great experiences in the future!

reviews.review_title: A beautiful game with great puzzles.
reviews.review_description:
The detailed rendering of 16th century Florence (cityscape and rooms) is nothing short of beautiful, and the lighting very artistic.  Several of the chapters were situated in places that I love to simply go back to sit and savor.    The limited number of teleport destinations made it easy to find the next part of each puzzle, but I wish I had been able to poke my head into more places and more deeply explore more of those beautiful sets. 

The puzzles were all rendered in excellent detail, and built with reasonable 16th century technology.  With so many different stations to visit in each chapter, the hints did a wonderful job of directing your attention to a next profitable step, without solving the problems for you.  In correct-settings puzzles, the information you needed to operate the mechanisms had already been provided ... if you were attentive enough to notice.   And can-you-solve-this puzzles could always be worked out without trial and error. Simply understand what the mechanisms do, and come up with a combination of those steps that led to the (generally obvious) goal.
Must play this game if you are fan of solving puzzles

developer_response.body: Thank you for saying that!

reviews.review_description: The story and puzzles are very nice, as well as the graphics. But if you play the game seated some bits are hard to reach and require frequent recentering, taking you out of the nice environment

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review and for playing! If you have any technical difficulties or need help, please get in touch with us via davinci@bluebraingames.com, we're happy to help. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Excellent graphics and sound. Exceptionally stunning locations. The controls are easy to use, the chapters (7) are just long enough, and most puzzles are easy if you pay attention to details. It is very comfortable as you teleport from area/puzzle to puzzle, etc...  I have no complaints, only a sincere wish for the next game to have many more chapters. 🥰

I'm on the last challenge of chapter two (I think it's the last one) and I'm done. I'm going to uninstall this game right after I leave this review. 
I like to think I'm decent at puzzle games. If the challenges are this complicated with only the vaguest of hints (showing me I need to slide a mechanism 100 times without ever giving me more detail is beyond frustrating). When a game is making me more annoyed than it is letting me enjoy it, that's when I wave the white flag. 
Maybe I'm just not smart enough for this one, I don't know. But I sure won't be sticking around to find out, nor will I ever attempt it again. I hope the rest of the players have better luck and more fun than I did. 
That being said, I will say the graphics were decent but I didn't get far enough in to have an opinion about the storyline.
I'm not in the habit of leaving reviews that aren't mostly positive, so I came back and bumped it up a star. Just because I didn't enjoy doesn't mean you won't, so give it a try if it's something you think you'd like. I didn't purchase the game as it was redeemable on Horizon+ at the time of this review.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to write such a thoughtful review. We appreciate you giving the game a chance and sharing your honest experience. We’re glad you enjoyed the visuals, and we especially appreciate that you updated your rating and encouraged others to try it for themselves. Thanks for playing.

reviews.review_description: What impressed me the most were the graphics. It’s like you’re actually in ancient Venice. Each environment is so rich and detailed including sounds. The puzzles were fun and included lots of hands on interaction. I liked the story as well and the hints were helpful and didn’t give too much away. I appreciated the special feature included on your wrist also. My only negative was I finished it, I was hoping it would go on and on.

Graphics, variety, & concept are excellent. Some of the stuff to figure out is extremely difficult & the clues are abysmal. They basically tell you where you should go. The problem is, you are already there but cannot figure out what combination of moves will put things in the right configuration. A skip option should be available if you get stuck for a prolonged amount of time.  This is much harder than Dark Room & Ghosttown, both of which had some difficult things to figure out but never felt impossible. I often quit in exasperation & wish I had not spent my money on this.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. We’re glad to hear you enjoyed the graphics, variety, and concept of the game.

We understand that some puzzles can be challenging and require careful observation and experimentation. The design is intended to encourage creative problem-solving, but we appreciate feedback about difficulty and skip options—it helps us consider ways to improve player experience in future updates.

We hope you’ll give the game another try and continue exploring its world. There are many moments that players find rewarding once they figure out the solutions.

Thank you for your support and for being part of our community!

reviews.review_description:
Absolutely amazing! This is a stunningly beautiful game with a feeling of total immersion. If you love high-quality quests and tactile puzzles, this is an absolute must-have.
The mechanisms in VR feel incredibly real — when you manually turn the gears and open the hidden drawers of Da Vinci's ancient inventions, you totally forget you're wearing a headset. Every room in the house is a masterpiece that you want to explore for hours.
I highly recommend this to all fans of escape rooms and complex puzzles. In my opinion, it’s even better and more grand than other titles in the genre. 10/10!
Some very challenging puzzles. Brilliant game
Good game not long enugh for the price culd have been made longer game play and to finish wher it duse is a pain

This game is pretty good for your mind. It helps you think more. But the reason why im giving ut a 4 stsr instead of 5 is because it doesnt have free movement. It doesnt matter if the game is my favorite videogame of all time. If it doesn't have free movement, j won't play it.

Wow!  This game takes it to a whole new level. Walk in the shoes of Da Vinci, lots of puzzles to solve. Does have a hint device which helps to a point. The only real criticism I have is that some of the puzzles, at first , don't make sense. And it took a bit of hunting to find the clues and sequences to them. But I LOVE this game and I think, if you want a fully immersive experience, then this game is for you.

I've played both House of Davinci games on Android and thoroughly enjoyed them. They're a great mix of puzzles and story. The VR House of Davinci is a great adaptation of the original and offers a beautiful immersive environment. It offers something to those who have played the game on other platforms as well as those who are new to the game. I'm jealous of those that are new to it. Well done Blue Brain! Can't wait for the sequel!

I love this game. I played it twice already. Very good graphics and sounds that take you way back in Italy in the era of Leonardo da Vinci! Good puzzles that make the cogs in your brain turn and a good, misterious story behind it. Good job! When is the sequel guys!! Come on :)

I decided to get this game after recently beating a Room VR: Dark Matter. While this one has some pretty cool features and graphics, I find it to be a bit glitchy and hard to navigate. I spent so much time trying to rotate and move puzzle pieces on a level that it became quite irritating because it wouldn’t recognize me trying to move a single piece and kept turning things. Also, getting items from your inventory is a bit more difficult than it needs to be.

I was really hoping this one would be up there with the Dark Matter game but it’s kind of left me feeling underwhelmed in comparison. Even the dialogue boxes seem kind of out of theme with the game. Don’t get me wrong, it’s still a cool puzzle game for sure and I don’t think I’d consider it money wasted.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your experience. We’re glad to hear you enjoyed the puzzles and graphics of the game.

We appreciate your feedback regarding controls and inventory navigation, as well as the dialogue boxes. We know how important smooth interaction is in VR, and your comments help us identify areas for improvement in future updates.

It’s great to hear you still found the game enjoyable overall, and we hope you continue to explore its world and uncover its challenges.

reviews.review_description: If you played The Room VR and liked it, you will like this too. Its good quality all round.
I'm 63 and I love this brain game. I think this game is well made and beautiful graphics.  Please make more.

The game is really beautiful, the puzzles are great, and the multi-step hints are well calibrated so you don't lose your mind, and without spoiling the puzzle solution (unless you are running out of ideas and go to the last one).

Fun and immersive, highly recommend!

I'm giving 5 stars even though I got bored with it soon into the game. I had to keep referring to the walkthrough to get me through the puzzles. That's not the Devs fault. Some brains are wired differently so this game for me just isn't enjoyable. I can see how clever the devs have been in developing this game. Where they got their ideas from is beyond me? It must have taken forever to build. So my 5 star is on appreciation for what they've achieved. I'll keep going in and out of the game but if I need a walkthrough then it becomes pointless.

What a great game. The machinery and moving parts!  Ah what a joy. 

I have to say that I had to look up one of the puzzles. Even when I knew where I needed to do something I could not figure out what to do.  In some cases it is a trial and error rather than consciously doing something.  But perhaps that is exactly what you would expect from da Vinci. 

Overall a lovely and truly enjoyable game for puzzle lovers
I had fun with this it was really fun and well crafted, My only problem is that the ending seems a bit all over the place a tiny bit confusing

I was too late to get a refund on this game which is unfortunate. After playing Ghost Town, The Room, Red Matter 2, I decided to buy this game due to reviews. Compared to the other games, I found it to be remarkably tedious in movements, the narrative and overall experience. My positioning had to be constantly corrected by pressing the meta button as you are unable to use controls to back away or move forward to a puzzle.  Maybe if this had been one of my first adventure puzzle game I wouldn't have been so disappointed.  For the cost of this game I expected more.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback. We genuinely appreciate it, even when the experience did not meet your expectations.

The House of Da Vinci VR was designed with a fixed-position, interaction-focused approach. Movement is intentionally limited and based on repositioning rather than free locomotion, primarily to ensure comfort and avoid motion sickness for a wide range of players. 

Needing to recenter via the Meta button is expected behavior in this design, but we understand that for experienced VR players it can feel tedious and break immersion.

While we cannot influence refund policies once the Meta refund window has passed, feedback like yours is actively reviewed and taken into account for future VR projects.

reviews.review_description:
Sadly this game gets worse the further you progress.
It looks great most of the time and has lots of details on a lot of mechanisms etc. related to the puzzles.

But the controls are a problem. 
Since the game decides if your hands want to point with one finger, or grab something, or do nothing at all, you have problems from time to time to do what you actually want to do, because your hands are "blocked" in the wrong stance. 
You have to actually change your position in the room sometimes to get your hand(s) free to interact the way you want them. So annoying!

And the puzzles range between easy "push 2 buttons at the same time" and stupidly frustrating "find the right pattern without any clue whatsoever". 
Why in the world would I just randomly guess the Fibonacci sequence for the correct combination of numbers without any hint in the game at all? 
At these puzzles you are stuck for a long time, while most are simple button presses, or pulling a lever - since the game already gives you the right hand position it is always very clear what to do here.

So all in all, as a puzzle game, it is only mediocre.
Sadly, because it looks great and the setpieces are really nice.

developer_response.body:
Hi Julesologe,
thanks for taking the time to share such a detailed and balanced review!

We’re really glad you enjoyed the visual quality and the craftsmanship of the environments and mechanisms. 

Your comments are super helpful and are being shared with the team, especially around interaction clarity and better hinting for more complex puzzles. Thanks again for giving the game a fair shot and for the thoughtful feedback.

reviews.review_description: This was a great experiences. Love the tricky puzzles. Hints are there if you need them. But I enjoyed the gameplay. The only thing I would change is free moving instead of point and shoot direction. Still giving 5 stars as this was amazing !! Well done

This game's graphics are absolutely positively BEAUTIFUL it is stunning and I love it. The only quarral I have about the game is that it is not free roaming like The 7th Guest VR Game. I REALLY wish it was cause i want to explore around and look at everything and some times my character is way to close to the puzzle and I can't back it up. But other than that I love this game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words, we’re really glad you enjoyed the visuals and atmosphere. We understand your point about movement and positioning. Your feedback on exploration and spacing is noted and helps us improve future updates.

reviews.review_description: Liked it but it had some puzzles that required guessing or process of elimination to solve towards the end and there are some glitchy parts.

I’m 52, I really enjoy challenging escape room/puzzle games. So far this had been my favorite. I’ve played 7th Guest, The Room, IEYTD trilogy, Red Matter I & II and a few others I’m forgetting. This one is excellent and I recommend it at any price.
I enjoyed playing this game..for me it was not easy or hard I might say It was moderate. I wish it was harder but overall nice game with amazing graphics.

My husband and I absolutely loved playing this game.  We kept the same pace so we could play it together even though it’s a single player.  Now we are trying to find something similar. Highly recommend!￼
It was an awesome game. I highly recommend it.

This has to be one of my top games ever! I'm only up to chapter 2 and I am hooked! The graphics are amazing and the puzzles are so much fun to solve!! I do wish you could free roam a bit as I would love to explore more of the house and area just to chill and relax in! I need to see what other games you have made as I am definitely a fan!

The graphics and environments are great. The puzzles were fun, clever, and a little challenging. I loved the mechanical sound effects. Only downside was the ending. It was abrupt and didn’t really make sense. I was hoping to get to meet Leonardo at the end.

reviews.review_title: Its ok.. but i want to love it
reviews.review_description:
I finally got this game while it's free with Horizon+, after having done all the other story puzzle games and just having finished Ghost Town just recently, my expectations were reallly set high, and this game also game super recommended and has amazing reviews

First this game it looks beautiful! The visuals and the designs are just stunning! This is sort of where it stops unfortunately and it really hurts me saying this because i want to love this game so much, but the controls are soo incredibly frustrating that playing the game almost feels like a chore. Your finger feels even more fidgety than using hand motion. If you're super patient and can get passed the controls, the puzzles are fun and the story is good 😊 

Im giving it 2 stars only for the reason that it feels totally unplayable for me. If the controls would be better i would say a solid 4. Ill be buying it on Steam instead and enjoy it that way instead

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the detailed review and for trying the game through Horizon+. Glad you enjoyed the visuals, puzzles, and story. Sorry it didn’t fully land for you on the controls side. If you ever want to share more details, feel free to reach out to support. Appreciate you giving it a try.

reviews.review_description: I don't get how this game has such amazing reviews, I REALLY wanted to like it but the only thing done right are the visuals, I LOVE puzzle games like the room and I expect u to die but this isn't it, no hate to the devs there's potential but i constantly have to look up a playthrough bc I've got no idea how to beat a puzzle, and instead of an "aha" moment when I realize what I did wrong it's more of an "are u kidding me that's so stupid" type of situation, some of the puzzles just make no sense, or things u need to pick up but it's far away and not highlighted at all so there's no possible way u would know to grab the item, there's also times where I was wondering what I needed to do and the solution was bend down and put on of the items in something that I literally COULDN'T SEE unless I bent down. I usually just try different solutions untill I beat a puzzle in games but sometimes the solution is so dumb it's insane, and it's not just that I'm personally bad at puzzle games bc I play alot of them and would say I'm at least decent at them. Good visuals but stupid, dumb and not at all logical puzzles (for a puzzle game the puzzles should be good, ofc lmao) and since the game has u teleporting around the different rooms and not free roam it's important that u have easy access to all interactable things in the game, but there's times I've had to recenter further to the back, left, right, I've even had to recenter standing on my chair so I didn't have to bend over during the puzzle Wich u can tell isn't very comfortable, stuff is constantly too close or too far away, I could go on but I gotta take a Sookie, visuals great puzzles mid/bad

developer_response.body: Thanks for the honest feedback and for giving it a real shot. Glad the visuals stood out and you spent time with it. We hear your points on the puzzles and pacing. If you want to share anything more specific, our support team is always here for you at davinci@bluebraingames.com. Appreciate you playing!

reviews.review_description: It’s a really fun game and it’s really pleasant to look at. However, one puzzle I have a serious complaint about and it’s the one where you have to line up one through four and you rotate them on a spinning circle. I started off wrong and couldn’t get them fixed. It became a puzzle within a puzzle and I had to quit the profile. I was genuinely having fun and then it made me feel retarded. I’ll start over and just cheat on that one petty puzzle.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your honest feedback. We’re glad to hear you found the game beautiful, challenging, and enjoyable overall.

We’re sorry that particular puzzle caused such a frustrating experience. Our goal is to create satisfying challenges, not moments that take away from the fun. Feedback like yours is valuable because it helps us understand where certain puzzles may need clearer logic or a better reset option.

Thank you for playing The House of Da Vinci VR!

reviews.review_description: the only thing i don't like with this app is that after i completed it i can't play again. the hands come back but the button that you use to see the hidden objects no longer is there. is there a way to fix this? i have tried uninstalling and reinstalling and it does not work. please help.

developer_response.body:
Hi there! What you are describing is not expected behavior. After completing the game, all core mechanics, including the hidden-object vision button, should remain available. Moreover, reinstalling the game normally resets all progress and restores everything.

To help us investigate this properly, could you please contact our support team directly at davinci@bluebraingames.com and include:
	•	Your headset model
	•	Whether you are using hand tracking or controllers
	•	Confirmation that the issue occurs immediately after a fresh reinstall, before starting a new game

We would be happy to look into this further and help you get back into the game.

reviews.review_description: This was an amazing puzzle game and one of the most immersive VR experiences I’ve played. The puzzles are clever, the atmosphere is beautiful, and everything feels polished and thoughtfully designed. You can really tell the developers put a lot of passion, creativity, and care into this game. It’s a fantastic experience from start to finish and absolutely deserves 5 stars.
The immersion is impressive. Lots of details and the textures are amazing. The puzzles are absolutely brilliant.

I have really enjoyed playing and would recommend it to others. There were a few issues with tracking occasionally, but nothing that would ruin the experience. I'm only sad that I have finished it now as it was great stepping into that world every night to escape. Will look out for any other games by these guy's. It must be really hard to make games like this and I feel sometimes people are very critical of minor things that constructive feedback could help sort out. Well done on making a great game!

The House of Da Vinci VR is a highly engaging game, the details are phenomenal! This game is a must. Absolutely recommend buying. I'm excited for the rest of the games to be released on VR! Can't wait!!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot, this is amazing to read. Really happy you enjoyed the details and the puzzles, the team puts a lot of care into that. Great to hear you’re excited for what’s next, we appreciate the support a lot.

reviews.review_description: Fantastic!!

The puzzles are great. Some seem to lack continuity and pathway. Come back with a longer game with mysteries to solve in order to get to the next stage. 
Nice work!

developer_response.body: We are totally with you – games like these are never just long enough! Thank you for playing.

reviews.review_description:
The graphics in this game are remarkable, with great detail! All the mechanical puzzles are remarkably created, fun to do, and thought inspiring! Enjoyed the story line.
This is a great game. Really enjoyed all the mechanical puzzles!
I like this game a lot! It’s beautiful and puzzles are not too easy and not to hard! Thanks!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, we appreciate your review!

reviews.review_description: played the first time on pc, VR version is INSANELY immersive and off the hook. A must buy for any puzzle game fan!
Amasing game. Interesting puzzles and biutiful graphic. I'm looking forward for new game from that team as it took me less then 6 hours to complete.
Thoroughly enjoyed the gameplay.  Highly recommend this game for those looking for puzzles. We need another installment.
This really is a beautifully designed game. It has detailed graphics and the puzzles really test you. I wish it had more levels but it was worth playing. Hopefully they add some more later.

I created a new save file twice now, played for a few hours, even went back to the home screen  to double check my file saved. However when I came back the next day to play again the save file was gone, so frustrating  this has happened to me twice now can't even get a refund.

developer_response.body:
We’re very sorry for the frustration this caused. Save files in The House of Da Vinci VR are stored locally on the headset, so if data is removed or corrupted by the system, they unfortunately cannot be restored.

If this keeps happening, please contact us directly at davinci@bluebraingames.com so we can look into your case.
